pi_items.source: Verified via airfoiltools.com and Stanford NACA airfoil documentation. NACA 23015 is a valid 5-digit series airfoil (design Cl=0.3, camber position 15%, thickness 15%)
Verified via QBlade documentation and airfoil analysis research. Reynolds 1,000,000 is within the typical operating range (50,000 to 1,000,000) for NACA 23015 airfoils in wind turbine applications
Verified via QBlade documentation (airfoil_analysis.html) and XFoil research. NCrit=9 is the default value for general wind turbine applications, corresponding to typical ambient disturbance levels
Verified via NACA 5-digit airfoil designation analysis. The first digit '2' in NACA 23015 means 2 × 0.15 = 0.3 design lift coefficient
Verified via NACA 5-digit format. The second digit '3' in NACA 23015 means 3 × 5% = 15% camber position
Verified via NACA designation. The last two digits '15' indicate 15% maximum thickness as percentage of chord
Verified via QBlade documentation and wind turbine aerodynamics literature. Mach=0 is standard for wind turbines (blade velocity much lower than speed of sound)
Verified via QBlade Forum and XFoil research. Angle of attack range -10° to +20° is within the aerodynamically important region and XFoil's accurate prediction zone
Verified via QBlade Forum and XFoil research. Angle of attack range -10° to +20° covers pre-stall conditions where XFoil is most accurate
Verified via QBlade Polar Extrapolation documentation. Viterna is the default and most extensively used method in QBlade for 360° polar extrapolation
Verified via QBlade documentation. .wpa is the historical project format. Modern QBlade (2.0+) uses .qpr (binary) format, but .wpa may still be supported for backward compatibility
Path is reasonable default for user 'ga' home directory, confirmed in setup_task.sh script which creates this directory structure

privileged_info_summary: The NACA 23015 is a valid 5-digit series airfoil with a design lift coefficient of 0.3, 15% camber position, and 15% maximum thickness. The specified Reynolds number of 1,000,000 is within the standard operating range for this airfoil. NCrit=9 is the standard default value for wind turbine applications. The Mach number of 0 is appropriate for wind turbine blades. The angle of attack range of -10° to +20° covers the aerodynamically important linear region where XFoil is most accurate. The 360° polar extrapolation using the Viterna method is a standard feature in QBlade for generating complete polar data needed for blade simulations. The output file format (.wpa) is a recognized QBlade project format (though newer versions use .qpr).
